key_insight: SHIFT is a missingness-aware transformer that can predict survival directly from incomplete genomic profiles, avoiding test-time imputation and reducing the need to drop patients or genes.
clinical_relevance: It could help precision oncology models generalize across hospitals with different sequencing panels, making multi-center survival prediction more practical.
method: Represents each genomic feature separately and applies masked self-attention.
Uses a feature-availability mask so predictions rely only on observed inputs.
Trains with variable-rate feature masking to handle heterogeneous missingness patterns.
evidence_signal: In glioblastoma and lung squamous cell carcinoma, including external validation across multiple cohorts with severe panel mismatch, SHIFT outperformed or matched standard survival baselines and imputation-based methods.
watch_item: Performance may still depend on how representative the training cohorts are, and real-world deployment requires careful handling of site-specific panel differences and missingness patterns.
paper_title: SHIFT: Survival Prediction from Incomplete and Heterogeneous Genomic Data
